aliases: The town of Our Lady the Queen of the Angels of the Little Portion
description: seat of Los Angeles County, and largest city in California, United States
description_wiki: Los Angeles (often referred to by its initials, LA) is the most populous city in the U.S. state of California, and the commercial, financial, and cultural center of Southern California.

description: neighborhood in Los Angeles, California, United States
description_wiki: Pacific Palisades is a neighborhood in the Westside region of the city of Los Angeles, California, situated about 20 miles (32 km) west of downtown.

description: country in north-west Europe, part of the United Kingdom
description_wiki: England is a country that is part of the United Kingdom.

description: high school in Santa Monica, Los Angeles County, California
description_wiki: Santa Monica High School, officially abbreviated to Samohi or SMHS, is a public high school in Santa Monica, California.

description: neighborhood in the northern central portion of the Westside region of Los Angeles, California, USA
description_wiki: Westwood is a commercial and residential neighborhood in the northern central portion of the Westside region of the city of Los Angeles, California.

aliases: Rodolfo Pietro Filiberto Raffaello Guglielmi di Valentina d'Antonguella
Rodolfo Alfonso Raffaello Pierre Filibert Guglielmi di Valentina d'Antonguolla
Rodolfo Alfonso Raffaello Piero Filiberto Guglielmi
description: Italian actor (1895–1926)
description_wiki: Rodolfo Pietro Filiberto Raffaele Guglielmi di Valentina d'Antonguolla (May 6, 1895 – August 23, 1926), known professionally as Rudolph Valentino or mononymously as Valentino was an Italian-born actor and dancer.

description: Wikimedia disambiguation page
scheduled publication containing news of events, articles, features, editorials, and advertising; online, in print, or (usually) both
description_wiki: A newspaper is a periodical publication containing written information about current events and is often typed in black ink with a white or gray background.

description: baseball team and Major League Baseball franchise in San Francisco, California, United States
description_wiki: The San Francisco Giants are an American professional baseball team based in San Francisco.

description: 1921 Major League Baseball championship series
description_wiki: The 1921 World Series was the championship series in Major League Baseball for the 1921 season.

description: German Zeppelin (rigid airship)
description_wiki: LZ 127 Graf Zeppelin (Deutsches Luftschiff Zeppelin 127) was a German passenger-carrying hydrogen-filled rigid airship that flew from 1928 to 1937.

description: 1937 airship accident in New Jersey
description_wiki: The Hindenburg disaster was an airship accident that occurred on May 6, 1937, in Manchester Township, New Jersey, United States.

description: airport in Santa Monica, California
description_wiki: Santa Monica Airport (IATA: SMO, ICAO: KSMO, FAA LID: SMO) (Santa Monica Municipal Airport) is a general aviation airport largely in Santa Monica, California, United States, in the Greater Los Angeles area.

description: 1921-1967 aerospace manufacturer in the United States
description_wiki: The Douglas Aircraft Company was an American aerospace and defense company that was based in Southern California.

description: thoroughfare in Santa Monica, Beverly Hills, and Los Angeles, United States
description_wiki: Wilshire Boulevard ( WIL-shər) is a prominent 15.83 mi (25.48 km) boulevard in the Los Angeles area of Southern California, extending from Ocean Avenue in the city of Santa Monica east to Grand Avenue in the Financial District of downtown Los Angeles.

description: Games of the X Olympiad, in Los Angeles, USA
description_wiki: The 1932 Summer Olympics (officially the Games of the X Olympiad and also known as Los Angeles 1932) were an international multi-sport event held from July 30 to August 14, 1932, in Los Angeles, California, United States.

description: stadium in Los Angeles, California, USA
description_wiki: The Los Angeles Memorial Coliseum (also known as the Los Angeles Coliseum or L.A. Coliseum) is a multi-purpose stadium in the Exposition Park neighborhood of Los Angeles, California, United States.

description: American murderer (1905-1998)
description_wiki: Winnie Ruth Judd (January 29, 1905 – October 23, 1998), born Winnie Ruth McKinnell, also known as Marian Lane, was a medical secretary in Phoenix, Arizona, who was accused of murdering her friends, Anne LeRoi and Hedvig “Sammy” Samuelson, in October 1931.

description: public, community college in Santa Monica, California, USA
description_wiki: Santa Monica College (SMC) is a public community college in Santa Monica, California.

description: American businessman (1911-1978)
description_wiki: William Fisk Harrah (September 2, 1911 – June 30, 1978) was an American businessman and the founder of Harrah's Hotel and Casinos, now part of Caesars Entertainment.

description: beachfront neighborhood in Los Angeles, California, USA
description_wiki: Venice is a neighborhood of the City of Los Angeles within the Westside region of Los Angeles County, California, United States.
